methodology: Axial Coding - Phase 3 Failure Taxonomy for Trace Analysis
description: Structured failure taxonomy for Phase 3 axial coding. Each failure category includes observed behaviors, frequency tracking, and evaluation type.

failure_categories.name: Failed Claim Construction
failure_categories.description: Claim elements are understood based on LLM's interpretation of the term rather than how the claim element is defined in the specification
observed_behaviors: Claim elements understood based on LLM's interpretation instead of specification
Breaking claim elements incorrectly to understand flow
Not using specification support to define claim terms
Large claims not properly decomposed into elements
failure_categories.impact: Element interpretation affects all downstream analysis

failure_categories.name: Absent PHOSITA Reasoning
failure_categories.description: The mental model of a person skilled in the art to determine obviousness is weak. Every novel element is considered non-obvious with no reasoning on why a PHOSITA would judge this as obvious or non-obvious
observed_behaviors: No reasoning for why PHOSITA would find element obvious or non-obvious
Missing consideration of ordinary skilled person's understanding
failure_categories.impact: Critical for obviousness assessment which affects patentability conclusion

failure_categories.name: Citation Text
failure_categories.description: Tool summarizes prior art text instead of quoting verbatim, preventing user verification of the mapping between prior art and claim elements
observed_behaviors: Corresponding text is summarized instead of quoted
User cannot verify the claimed correspondence
Paraphrasing prevents traceability back to source
failure_categories.impact: Reduces verifiability and traceability of mapping

failure_categories.name: Inconsistent Verdict Logic
failure_categories.description: Difference between element mapping and verdict. Element is mapped as novel but the final opinion is that it is anticipated (or other contradictions)
observed_behaviors: Element mapped as novel but verdict says anticipated
failure_categories.impact: Indicates logical inconsistency in analysis that affects conclusion reliability

failure_categories.name: Claim Elements Evaluated Unnecessarily
failure_categories.description: Tool runs novelty check on generic preamble, input elements, or preprocessing steps that should not be evaluated for inventive step
observed_behaviors: Generic preamble analyzed as novel element
Input elements checked for novelty incorrectly
Steps that are foundational treated as inventive
failure_categories.impact: Inflates novelty assessment by evaluating non-patentable subject matter

phase_3_workflow.step_1_identify: Open trace and identify which of the 5 failure categories apply
phase_3_workflow.step_2_mark: Select all applicable failure categories (can be 1-5)
phase_3_workflow.step_3_document: Write comment explaining why each selected failure mode applies

usage_notes.pass_verdict: Use PASS when trace has no significant failures - failure_modes field will be empty
usage_notes.fail_verdict: Use FAIL when trace has ≥1 failure - select applicable categories
usage_notes.multiple_modes: A single trace can have 1-5 failure modes - select all that apply
usage_notes.dimension_context: Use trace dimensions (claim_type, claim_length, relationship) to understand error patterns
usage_notes.open_coded_preservation: Original Phase 1 open-coded modes are preserved for traceability

analysis_instructions.error_patterns: After Phase 3 annotation, group failures by: (1) failure category, (2) trace dimensions, (3) verdict
analysis_instructions.hotspots: Identify which claim types/lengths most frequently trigger specific failure modes
analysis_instructions.improvement_areas: Focus prompt improvements on highest-frequency failure categories
analysis_instructions.baseline: 11 Phase 1 traces auto-mapped to Phase 3; ~76 unannotated traces remaining
